concept: Patient / Willing to Wait / Enduring Without Complaint
description: Able to accept or tolerate delays, problems, or suffering without becoming annoyed or anxious. 'Hurao' names the quality of peaceful waiting—endurance without agitation, acceptance of time's own pace.
sound_symbolism: The word waits with ease. 'Hu' begins with holding breath, willingness to pause. Then 'ra' remains steady, continuing presence. Finally 'o' opens to acceptance, waiting without strain.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'hurao shiro' (patient tree). Related to 'kolu' (steady) but emphasizes willingness to wait rather than constancy of state.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Patience (khanti) is a paramita—perfection developed through practice, accepting what arises without reactivity.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Patience was essential to traditional life—waiting for seasons, for growth, for processes that cannot be rushed.
